calls.content:
### Work Breakdown Structure

- **Backend Development**  
  - Design database schema for reviews, ratings, and photos (2 person days)  
  - Implement API endpoints for submitting, retrieving, and moderating reviews (5 person days)  
  - Integrate photo storage solution (e.g., AWS S3) (3 person days)  
  - Develop moderation queue functionality (4 person days)  
  - Implement average rating calculation logic (2 person days)  

- **Frontend Development**  
  - Create UI for submitting reviews (star rating, text, photo uploads) (4 person days)  
  - Develop review display component on product pages (3 person days)  
  - Implement average rating display on product pages (2 person days)  
  - Build moderation dashboard for admin users (5 person days)  

- **Testing and QA**  
  - Write unit tests for backend APIs (3 person days)  
  - Perform end-to-end testing of review submission and display (4 person days)  
  - Test moderation workflow and edge cases (3 person days)  
  - Conduct performance testing for photo uploads (2 person days)  

- **Deployment and Integration**  
  - Deploy backend changes to production environment (2 person days)  
  - Deploy frontend changes to production environment (2 person days)  
  - Monitor post-launch performance and fix bugs (3 person days)  

- **Project Management**  
  - Sprint planning and daily standups (5 person days)  
  - Documentation and handover (3 person days)  

### Total Estimate  
51 person days  

### Assumptions  
- The company uses a cloud-based infrastructure (e.g., AWS) for photo storage.  
- A pre-existing admin dashboard framework is available for moderation features.  
- The holiday season deadline is 6 weeks from the start of the project.  
- A team of 3 developers (1 backend, 1 frontend, 1 full-stack) is available full-time.  
- No major changes to the existing product page layout are required.  
- Photo uploads are limited to 3 per review, with a maximum size of 5MB per photo.  
- Moderation is a manual process handled by internal staff.
